Action.description:
A single action in a rule's 'then' block.

Must have exactly one action-type key. Available actions:
  set             — set a decision output to a value
  add_reason      — append a Reason to a list-typed decision
  add_instruction — append an Instruction to a list-typed decision
  add_to_set      — add a value to a set-typed decision
  append_to_list  — append a value to a list-typed decision

add_reason.description: Append a Reason to a list-typed decision (typically 'reasons').

set.description: Set a decision output to a specific value, e.g. {eligible: true}.
set.title: Set

add_instruction.description: Append an Instruction to a list-typed decision.

add_to_set.description: Add a value to a set-typed decision.
add_to_set.title: Add To Set

append_to_list.description: Append a value to a list-typed decision.
append_to_list.title: Append To List

Action.title: Action

citations.description: Legal citations supporting this denial reason.
citations.title: Citations

Citation.description: A legal or policy citation supporting a rule or reason.

label.description: Citation label, e.g. '42 USC § 1437a' or '7 CFR § 273.9'.
label.title: Label

excerpt.description: Brief excerpt from the cited text (optional).
excerpt.title: Excerpt

Citation.title: Citation

ComputedField.description:
A derived intermediate value (CIVIL v2+). Supports optional tags (CIVIL v3).

Computed fields are NOT primary decision outputs — they are intermediate values
available to rules (in 'when:' expressions) and to other computed fields.
Define in dependency order: no forward references allowed.

Each field must have exactly one of 'expr', 'conditional', 'invoke' (CIVIL v4),
or 'table_lookup' (CIVIL v7).

type.description: Value type: money, bool, float, int, string, or object (for invoke: fields).
type.title: Type

module.description: Sub-module name for invoke: fields (no extension). E.g. 'income_calc'.
module.title: Module

currency.description: Currency code for money type, e.g. 'USD'.
currency.title: Currency

description.description: Human-readable explanation of what this field computes.
description.title: Description

source.description: Policy document location where this computed field is derived from, e.g. '7 CFR § 273.9(d)(1) — Earned Income Deduction'.
source.title: Source

expr.description: CIVIL expression producing the computed value. Mutually exclusive with 'conditional' and 'invoke'. May reference fact fields, constants, other computed fields, and table lookups. In computed: context, max(a, b) and min(a, b) are also available.
expr.title: Expr

conditional.description: If/then/else branch for conditional computed values. Mutually exclusive with 'expr' and 'invoke'.

invoke.description: (CIVIL v4) Invoke an external sub-module as a sub-computation. Mutually exclusive with 'expr' and 'conditional'. Requires 'module:' and 'type: object'.

table_lookup.description: (CIVIL v7) Declarative table lookup. Resolves key column names from in-scope computed fields or entity fields. Mutually exclusive with 'expr', 'conditional', and 'invoke'.

review.description: Extraction quality scores for this computed field.

tags.description: (CIVIL v3) Optional list of string labels. Currently only 'output' has defined behavior: marks the field for exposure as a Catala `output` declaration (and subsequently in the demo API's ComputedBreakdown). Ignored by the Rego transpiler. Example: tags: [output]
tags.title: Tags

ComputedField.title: ComputedField

Conditional.description:
An if/then/else expression for computed fields.

Use instead of 'expr' when the computed value depends on a boolean condition.
All three branches (if, then, else) are required.

if.description: Boolean CIVIL expression — condition to test.
if.title: If

then.description: CIVIL expression for the true branch.
then.title: Then

else.description: CIVIL expression for the false branch.
else.title: Else

Conditional.title: Conditional

DecisionField.description: An output decision value produced by rule evaluation.

default.description: Default value when no rules fire, e.g. false for bool or [] for list.
default.title: Default

description.description: Description of what this decision represents.
description.title: Description

item.description: Item type for list/set decisions, e.g. 'Reason' or 'string'.
item.title: Item

values.description: Allowed values for enum decisions, e.g. ['approve', 'deny', 'manual_verification'].
values.title: Values

expr.description: CIVIL expression computing this decision value directly. For bool decisions, overrides the default count(reasons)==0 logic. Mutually exclusive with 'conditional'.
expr.title: Expr

DecisionField.title: DecisionField

Effective.description: Effective date range for this ruleset.

end.description: Effective end date (YYYY-MM-DD) — optional for time-bounded policies.
end.title: End

Effective.title: Effective

FactEntity.description:
An input entity (e.g., Household, Applicant) with named typed fields.

Entity names use PascalCase. Field names use snake_case.

fields.description: Named fields of this entity (snake_case → field definition).
fields.title: Fields

FactEntity.title: FactEntity

FactField.description: A single typed field within a fact entity.

type.description: Data type. Primitives: int, float, bool, string, date, money. Complex: list, set, enum. Custom: any type defined in the 'types' section.
type.title: Type

source.description: Policy document location where this field is defined, e.g. '7 CFR § 273.9(a) — Income and Deductions'.
source.title: Source

optional.description: Whether this field may be absent in input (default: false = always required).
optional.title: Optional

currency.description: Currency code for money type, e.g. 'USD'.
currency.title: Currency

values.description: Valid values for enum type, e.g. ['single', 'mfj', 'mfs'].
values.title: Values

FactField.title: FactField

InvokeField.description:
Input binding for an invoke: computed field (CIVIL v4).

Maps sub-module entity names to parent entity names.
Key = sub-module entity name; value = parent entity name.

bind.description: Entity binding: sub-module entity name → parent entity name. E.g., {Household: ClientData} routes the parent's ClientData entity to the sub-module's Household input. At least one entry required.
bind.title: Bind

InvokeField.title: InvokeField

Jurisdiction.description: Jurisdiction metadata for a CIVIL module.

level.description: Jurisdiction level: federal, state, county, or city.
level.title: Level

Jurisdiction.title: Jurisdiction

ReviewBlock.description:
Inline extraction quality scores. All four score fields are required when present.

Score scale: 1 = very low, 3 = moderate, 5 = very high.
Notes are required for any score ≤ 2 or ≥ 4. Scores have no effect on
transpilation or OPA evaluation — they exist only for human review.

extraction_fidelity.description: 1–5: how accurately the AI captured the policy intent. 1 = guessed (source is silent), 3 = reasonable translation, 5 = direct quote or explicit formula.
extraction_fidelity.title: Extraction Fidelity

source_clarity.description: 1–5: how clear and unambiguous the source policy text was. 1 = contradictory or absent, 3 = reasonably clear with minor ambiguity, 5 = exact thresholds or formulas stated verbatim.
source_clarity.title: Source Clarity

logic_complexity.description: 1–5: number of conditions, boolean depth, and table lookups. 1 = single boolean or comparison, 3 = 4–6 conditions or 1 table lookup, 5 = 10+ conditions, nested booleans, multiple tables.
logic_complexity.title: Logic Complexity

policy_complexity.description: 1–5: density of legalese, cross-references, and exceptions. 1 = plain everyday English, 3 = moderate legalese or defined terms, 5 = dense statutory language, exceptions-to-exceptions, multi-CFR cross-refs.
policy_complexity.title: Policy Complexity

notes.description: Explanation for flagged items. Required for any score ≤ 2 or ≥ 4. May be omitted if all four scores are 3.
notes.title: Notes

ReviewBlock.title: ReviewBlock

Rule.description:
A single deny or allow rule.

Rules are evaluated according to the rule_set.precedence strategy.
The 'when' condition is a boolean CIVIL expression; if it evaluates to true,
all 'then' actions are executed.

CIVIL v6 annotation fields (group, mutex_group) are maintainability metadata only.
They have no effect on transpilation or rule evaluation — analogous to 'review'.

id.description: Unique rule identifier. Recommended format: '<JURISDICTION>-<TOPIC>-<KIND>-<SEQ>', e.g. 'FED-SNAP-DENY-001'.
id.title: Id

kind.description: Rule type: 'deny' to deny eligibility, 'allow' to grant it.
kind.title: Kind

priority.description: Evaluation priority — lower number = higher priority. Allow rules typically use priority 100+.
priority.title: Priority

when.description: Boolean CIVIL expression — the condition under which this rule fires. May reference fact fields (Household.field), constants, and computed fields. See module docstring for expression language reference.
when.title: When

then.description: Actions to take when the 'when' condition is true. Must be non-empty.
then.title: Then

description.description: Optional human-readable description of this rule.
description.title: Description

source.description: Policy document location where this rule is defined, e.g. '7 CFR § 273.9(a)(1) — Gross Income Test'.
source.title: Source

review.description: Extraction quality scores for this rule.

group.description: (CIVIL v6) Workflow stage this rule belongs to. Must match a name in rule_set.workflow_stages when that list is non-empty. Transpiler no-op — annotation only.
group.title: Group

mutex_group.description: (CIVIL v6) Mutual-exclusion group name. Rules sharing a mutex_group represent competing alternatives — only one should fire per evaluation. All rules in the same mutex_group must have unique priority values. Transpiler no-op — annotation only.
mutex_group.title: Mutex Group

Rule.title: Rule

RuleSet.description: Rule set configuration controlling evaluation strategy.

precedence.description: Evaluation strategy. deny_overrides_allow: any deny wins; allow_overrides_deny: any allow wins; first_match: first matching rule wins; priority_order: evaluate in priority order.
precedence.title: Precedence

workflow_stages.description: (CIVIL v6) Named evaluation phases for this rule set. Defines the controlled vocabulary for rule group: annotations. E.g., income_test, asset_test, household_composition. When present, validate_civil.py checks that every rule group: value matches a stage name defined here.
workflow_stages.title: Workflow Stages

RuleSet.title: RuleSet

TableDef.description:
A lookup table mapping key values to output values.

Tables are referenced in CIVIL expressions as:
  table('table_name', key_expr).value_column

description.description: Description of what this table represents.
description.title: Description

source.description: Policy document location where this table is defined, e.g. '7 CFR § 273.9(a)(1) — Gross Income Limits Table'.
source.title: Source

key.description: Key column name(s) used for lookup.
key.title: Key

value.description: Value column name(s) returned by lookup.
value.title: Value

rows.description: Table data rows as a list of dicts.
rows.title: Rows

TableDef.title: TableDef

TableLookupDef.description:
Declarative table lookup for a computed field (CIVIL v7).

Resolves key column names from in-scope computed fields (bare name) or
entity fields (Entity.field). Value column is inferred when the table
has exactly one value column; otherwise 'value' must be specified.

table.description: Name of the table to look up (must exist in tables:).
table.title: Table

key.description: Key column names (positional). Each name is resolved from in-scope computed fields or entity fields.
key.title: Key

value.description: Value column to return. Required when the table has multiple value columns; omit for single-value tables.
value.title: Value

TableLookupDef.title: TableLookupDef

WorkflowStage.description:
A named phase of policy evaluation (CIVIL v6).

Defines the controlled vocabulary for rule group: annotations within this module.
E.g., income_test, asset_test, household_composition.

description.description: One-sentence description of this evaluation phase.
description.title: Description

WorkflowStage.title: WorkflowStage

description:
Top-level CIVIL DSL module. One file per program or policy area.

CIVIL (Civic Instructions & Validations Intermediate Language) is designed
for government policy/regulation logic with full traceability to source law.

Required sections: module, description, version, jurisdiction, effective,
                   facts, decisions, rule_set, rules.
Optional sections: tables, constants, computed, types.

facts.description: Input fact types. Keys are entity names (PascalCase), values are entity definitions with typed fields.
facts.title: Facts

decisions.description: Output decision values produced by rule evaluation. Common pattern: eligible (bool) + reasons (list[Reason]).
decisions.title: Decisions

tables.description: Lookup tables for threshold values, e.g. income limits by household size. Reference in expressions as: table('table_name', key_expr).value_column
tables.title: Tables

constants.description: Named constant values (UPPER_SNAKE_CASE). Examples: MIN_AGE, FEDERAL_POVERTY_LEVEL, EARNED_INCOME_RATE.
constants.title: Constants

computed.description: Derived intermediate values (CIVIL v2). Use for multi-step formulas where each step depends on prior results (e.g., a deduction chain). Define in dependency order — no forward references. Computed field names are available as bare identifiers in 'when:' clauses.
computed.title: Computed

types.description: Custom type definitions (PascalCase). Built-in types Reason, Citation, and Instruction are available without definition.
types.title: Types

title: CivilModule
